column2: Iced Americano. Example: 아이스 아메리카노 주세요 = Iced Americano, please.
Short word for iced Americano. Example: 아아 하나 주세요 = One iced Americano, please.
One. Example: 하나 주세요 = One, please.
Takeout or to go. Example: 포장이요 = To go, please.
Receipt. Example: 영수증 주세요 = Receipt, please.
How much. Example: 이거 얼마예요? = How much is this?
Alone or by myself. Example: 혼자 볼게요 = I will look by myself.
Use after a noun to answer or order politely. Example: 포장이요 = To go, please.
Use for I will or a soft plan. Example: 먹고 갈게요 = I will eat here.
One iced Americano to go. Situation: Order iced Americano to go.
For here. Situation: Tell the barista you will stay.
How much is this? Situation: Ask the price while shopping.

column1: 괜찮아요, 저 혼자 볼게요 (gwaen-chan-a-yo jeo hon-ja bol-ge-yo)
column2: I am fine, I will look by myself. Situation: Shop staff asks if you need help.
I am fine, no thanks. Use when: Someone offers something you do not want.
I do not have one. Use when: Asked about points card or membership.
To go, please. Situation: Barista asks here or to go.

column1: 따뜻한 거 하나 주세요 (tta-tteut-han geo ha-na ju-se-yo)
column2: One hot drink, please. Situation: Order a hot drink.
Points or rewards. Example: 포인트 적립 하시나요? = Do you collect points?
This one, please. Situation: Point at an item and order.
Card. Example: 카드 결제요 = Card payment, please.
